title: Tomato Sauce on Carpet
stainChemistry: Same combination chemistry as tomato-sauce-cotton but on carpet the construction risks come in. Face yarn (nylon, olefin, polyester, wool) is where the stain sits; pooled sauce soaks to the backing and pad where it wicks back up as the carpet dries. tomato red pigment is fat-soluble — oil carrier first, then color work. Hot water on tomato-sauce-and-oil-carrier drives pigment deeper into the fiber AND activates backing adhesive on carpet. Cold only, damp cloth only, extraction between every pass.
whyThisWorks: Scrape solid bulk. Cold extract. NSD via damp cloth pulls the oil carrier and suspends surface pigment. Acid-side tannin spotter disrupts the tomato-tannin bond. 3% H₂O₂ + UV (if possible) oxidizes the residual tomato red pigment. Weighted dry towel between every wet pass protects the pad. Walked-on + dried tomato sauce on light carpet often plateaus at orange shadow and a pro call is the right answer.
defaultAssumption: Assume synthetic face yarn (nylon / olefin / polyester). Wool carpet + tomato sauce escalates. Aged tomato sauce (walked-on, dried in) climbs pretty quickly because oil-deep-in-backing + pigment = two problems.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dull blade, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape solid sauce off with a dull blade. Press a folded white towel straight down on the spot — don't rub. Outside-in. Lift and dry section, press again.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cold water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply cold NSD via damp cloth. Press, lift, rotate. Never pour. Weighted dry towel 5 min on top to extract. 2–4 cycles. NSD handles the oil carrier AND suspends surface pigment.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper. Disrupts the tomato tannin bond. Never pour. Dwell 3–5 min, blot.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 3% H₂O₂, sunlight if portable carpet piece
spottingProtocol.instruction: 3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop on residual orange-red. Dwell 5–10 min, blot. One oxidizer at a time.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp cloth to clear residual chemistry. Weighted dry towel 30 min extraction.
Fan the spot. Don't walk on it. Inspect next day for wicking — if orange ring returns, pad is contaminated.

steps: 2. Cold NSD via damp cloth, extract between every pass — handles oil AND pigment.
3. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A) by dropper.
4. 3% H₂O₂ on residual tomato red pigment.
5. NSD-damp flush + 30 min weighted dry towel.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
warnings: Never pour or pool liquid on carpet
Never use hot water — sets tomato red pigment + activates backing adhesive
Never skip extraction between wet passes

homeSolutions.instruction: Scrape solid sauce off with a dull blade or spoon edge. Don't rub. Then press a thick stack of white paper towels or a folded white cloth straight down on the spot. Lift, dry section, press. Work outside-in.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Cool Water on a DAMP Cloth — Never Pour
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water + a drop of Dawn. Press onto the spot, lift, rotate. Dawn does double duty here — strips the oil carrier AND lifts surface pigment. Never pour water on carpet; it goes straight to the pad and wicks orange back up as the carpet dries.
Mix 1 tsp white vinegar + 3 tbsp cool water. Dab on residual red with a damp cloth. Let sit 3 min. Blot.

homeSolutions.agent: 3% Hydrogen Peroxide for Orange Shadow (Whites / Light Carpet)
homeSolutions.instruction: For residual orange shadow on light-colored carpet: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide on the spot, let it bubble 5–10 min, blot. Patch-test a hidden area first on dark or patterned carpet — peroxide can lighten carpet dye.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia — toxic fume risk.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel Between Every Wet Pass
homeSolutions.instruction: After each wet pass, put a thick DRY towel on the spot with a heavy book or pan on top for 20–30 min. Pulls moisture UP out of the pad. Non-negotiable for carpet.
Fan the spot. Don't walk on it until bone-dry. Inspect next day in daylight. If an orange ring wicks back, the pad is contaminated and a pro extraction is the call.

Escalate for wool carpet, wicking, patch-test failure, or two rounds without clearing. Tell them: "Tomato sauce on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% peroxide + weighted towel."

steps: 1. Scrape solid sauce. Blot press straight down. No rub.
2. Cold water + Dawn on a DAMP cloth — never pour.
3. Diluted white vinegar dab for residual red.
4. 3% hydrogen peroxide on orange shadow (light carpet, patch-test first).
5. Weighted dry towel 20–30 min between every wet pass.
7. Escalate for wool, wicking, or plateau.
products: Heavy book or pan for weighted drying
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop for wool carpet, wicking, patch-test failure, or two-round plateau.
warnings: NEVER rub — drives pigment into pad
Cold only — hot water sets tomato red pigment + activates backing adhesive
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never hot water — sets tomato red pigment and backing adhesive
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / olefin / polyester face yarn: tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂

escalation.when: Wool carpet, stain wicks back, patch-test fails, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Tomato sauce on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% H₂O₂ + weighted towel."

contextQuestions: Carpet fiber — nylon, olefin, polyester, wool?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: tomato red pigment + oil on carpet = double problem. Aged sauce in the pad wicks back up as the carpet dries. Honest escalation threshold is lower on carpet than on fabric.
